questions.question: What is the difference between a constructor and a destructor?
expectedPoints: Constructor initializes objects, destructor cleans up resources
Constructor has same name as class, destructor has ~ prefix
Constructor can have parameters, destructor cannot
Constructor is called on creation, destructor on destruction

questions.question: Explain the various types of constructors with examples.
expectedPoints: Default constructor: no parameters
Parameterized constructor: takes parameters for initialization
Copy constructor: creates copy of existing object
Example syntax for each type

questions.question: What is constructor overloading and why is it useful?
expectedPoints: Multiple constructors with different parameter lists
Provides flexibility in object initialization
Allows objects to be created in different ways
Example demonstrating overloading

questions.question: Describe the order of constructor and destructor calls with examples.
expectedPoints: Constructors called in order of object creation
Destructors called in reverse order (LIFO)
Global objects constructed before main(), destroyed after
Local objects constructed when declared, destroyed when out of scope
Example code demonstrating execution order

questions.question: Explain the Rule of Three and its importance in C++ class design.
expectedPoints: Definition: if you need destructor/copy constructor/copy assignment, need all three
Prevents shallow copying issues
Ensures proper resource management
Example class demonstrating the rule
Consequences of violating the rule

questions.question: What are constructor initialization lists and when should they be used?
expectedPoints: Syntax: ClassName(params) : member1(value1), member2(value2) {}
More efficient than assignment in constructor body
Required for const members and references
Required for base class initialization
Example demonstrating proper usage
